title: subnet in podman_network as list
body:
### Feature request description

According to documentation and code subnet in podman_network is a string. Podman itself allows to pass subnet multiple times so it should be list of cidrs
`subnet=dict(type='str', required=False),`

```
  subnet:
    description:
      - Subnet in CIDR format
    type: str
```

### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Alternative is to create multiple networks

### Additional context

I am testing case with ipvlan with l3

comments.body: `Subnet in CIDR format` yields no match in this repo?! Are you talking about podman-py? Then you should file the issue against podman-py not podman.

Podman py's code does not match too. I am guessing you might be talking about the Ansible's [podman_network module](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/collections/containers/podman/podman_network_module.html#parameter-net_config/subnet). can you confirm @rkozlo ?
Yeaah... Sorry for that. I had several tabs opened and created issue in wrong repository...
